operation.title: Operation
operation.description: scan = scrape + rank competitors and return the winning picks (default). report = assemble the AI's packaging breakdowns into a self-contained HTML report.

competitorHandles.title: Competitor channels (scan)
competitorHandles.description: YouTube @handles or channel URLs of the competitors to scan. Only the first 'Max channels' are used. If empty, the actor looks for a competitor-list.md file in the working directory (handy for local runs).

maxChannels.title: Max channels (scan)
maxChannels.description: How many of the provided handles to actually scan.

uploadsPerChannel.title: Uploads per channel (scan)
uploadsPerChannel.description: How many of each channel's newest uploads to pull (used for ranking and to compute the channel's view median for the breakout score).

recencyDays.title: Recency window in days (scan)
recencyDays.description: Only videos published within this many days are eligible to be selected as winners.

topN.title: Top N by views/day (scan)
topN.description: How many recent videos to select purely by views-per-day. Breakout outliers are added on top of these.

breakoutMultiplier.title: Breakout multiplier (scan)
breakoutMultiplier.description: Any recent video whose views exceed this multiple of its channel's median views is also selected as a breakout outlier (e.g. 3 = more than 3x the channel median).

metadataConcurrency.title: Metadata concurrency (scan)
metadataConcurrency.description: How many video-metadata fetches to run in parallel.

reportData.title: Report data (report)
reportData.description: For operation=report. The scan output augmented by the AI: { pattern: string, picks: [ { ...pick, analysis: {...} } ], handles?, recencyDays?, totalVideosAnalyzed? }.

openReportLocally.title: Open report locally (report)
openReportLocally.description: On local runs only, open the generated HTML report in your default browser. Ignored on the Apify platform.

proxyConfiguration.title: Proxy configuration
proxyConfiguration.description: Proxy used for the YouTube requests. A residential proxy is recommended at scale to avoid being rate-limited.
